ai_content_json.method: 构建多状态强化学习的并行老虎机架构（PBRL），利用激光混沌时间序列替代伪随机数，通过Cart-Pole平衡问题验证其性能。
ai_content_json.result: PBRL在激光混沌驱动下比Q学习更快适应环境，且学习过程中状态分布特性存在显著差异。
ai_content_json.conclusion: PBRL算法通过利用混沌序列相关性，可加速强化学习过程，该方法对光子计算及其他计算平台均具普适价值。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于激光混沌的并行老虎机架构（PBRL），用于改进强化学习的决策效率。
motivation: 传统Q学习算法因结构限制难以适配光子系统，而光子学的高速特性可提升强化学习性能，需设计适合光子实现的新型架构。
method: 构建多状态强化学习的并行老虎机架构（PBRL），利用激光混沌时间序列替代伪随机数，通过Cart-Pole平衡问题验证其性能。
result: PBRL在激光混沌驱动下比Q学习更快适应环境，且学习过程中状态分布特性存在显著差异。
conclusion: PBRL算法通过利用混沌序列相关性，可加速强化学习过程，该方法对光子计算及其他计算平台均具普适价值。
search_text: Parallel bandit architecture based on laser chaos for reinforcement learning 基于激光混沌的强化学习并行老虎机架构 Accelerating artificial intelligence by photonics is an active field of study aiming to exploit the unique properties of photons. Reinforcement learning is an important branch of machine learning, and photonic decision-making principles have been demonstrated with respect to the multi-armed bandit problems. However, reinforcement learning could involve a massive number of states, unlike previously demonstrated bandit problems where the number of states is only one. Q-learning is a well-known approach in reinforcement learning that can deal with many states. The architecture of Q-learning, however, does not fit well photonic implementations due to its separation of update rule and the action selection. In this study, we organize a new architecture for multi-state reinforcement learning as a parallel array of bandit problems in order to benefit from photonic decision-makers, which we call parallel bandit architecture for reinforcement learning or PBRL in short. Taking a cart-pole balancing problem as an instance, we demonstrate that PBRL adapts to the environment in fewer time steps than Q-learning. Furthermore, PBRL yields faster adaptation when operated with a chaotic laser time series than the case with uniformly distributed pseudorandom numbers where the autocorrelation inherent in the laser chaos provides a positive effect. We also find that the variety of states that the system undergoes during the learning phase exhibits completely different properties between PBRL and Q-learning. The insights obtained through the present study are also beneficial for existing computing platforms, not just photonic realizations, in accelerating performances by the PBRL algorithms and correlated random sequences. 通过光子学加速人工智能是一个活跃的研究领域，旨在利用光子的独特特性。强化学习是机器学习的一个重要分支，光子决策原理已经针对多臂老虎机问题得到了证明。然而，强化学习可能涉及大量状态，这与之前演示的状态数量只有一个的强盗问题不同。 Q-learning 是强化学习中一种众所周知的方法，可以处理多种状态。然而，Q-learning 的架构由于更新规则和动作选择的分离而不太适合光子实现。在这项研究中，我们组织了一种新的多状态强化学习架构作为老虎机问题的并行阵列，以便从光子决策者中受益，我们将其称为强化学习的并行老虎机架构或简称 PBRL。以车杆平衡问题为例，我们证明了 PBRL 比 Q-learning 能够以更少的时间步数来适应环境。此外，当使用混沌激光时间序列操作时，PBRL 比使用均匀分布的伪随机数的情况产生更快的适应，在均匀分布的伪随机数中，激光混沌中固有的自相关性提供了积极的效果。我们还发现，系统在学习阶段经历的各种状态在 PBRL 和 Q 学习之间表现出完全不同的属性。通过本研究获得的见解对于现有计算平台（而不仅仅是光子实现）在加速 PBRL 算法和相关随机序列的性能方面也有益。 本文提出了一种基于激光混沌的并行老虎机架构（PBRL），用于改进强化学习的决策效率。 Takashi Urushibara,Nicolas Chauvet,Satoshi Kochi,Satoshi Sunada,Kazutaka Kanno,Atsushi Uchida,Ryoichi Horisaki,Makoto Naruse cs.ET,cs.LG,physics.app-ph

authors_text: Yuxin Zhang,Fan Tang,Weiming Dong,Haibin Huang,Chongyang Ma,Tong-Yee Lee,Changsheng Xu
title: Domain Enhanced Arbitrary Image Style Transfer via Contrastive Learning
title_zh: 通过对比学习增强领域任意图像风格迁移
abstract: In this work, we tackle the challenging problem of arbitrary image style transfer using a novel style feature representation learning method. A suitable style representation, as a key component in image stylization tasks, is essential to achieve satisfactory results. Existing deep neural network based approaches achieve reasonable results with the guidance from second-order statistics such as Gram matrix of content features. However, they do not leverage sufficient style information, which results in artifacts such as local distortions and style inconsistency. To address these issues, we propose to learn style representation directly from image features instead of their second-order statistics, by analyzing the similarities and differences between multiple styles and considering the style distribution. Specifically, we present Contrastive Arbitrary Style Transfer (CAST), which is a new style representation learning and style transfer method via contrastive learning. Our framework consists of three key components, i.e., a multi-layer style projector for style code encoding, a domain enhancement module for effective learning of style distribution, and a generative network for image style transfer. We conduct qualitative and quantitative evaluations comprehensively to demonstrate that our approach achieves significantly better results compared to those obtained via state-of-the-art methods. Code and models are available at https://github.com/zyxElsa/CAST_pytorch
summary: In this work, we tackle the challenging problem of arbitrary image style transfer using a novel style feature representation learning method. A suitable style representation, as a key component in image stylization tasks, is essential to achieve satisfactory results. Existing deep neural network based approaches achieve reasonable results with the guidance from second-order statistics such as Gram matrix of content features. However, they do not leverage sufficient style information, which results in artifacts such as local distortions and style inconsistency. To address these issues, we propose to learn style representation directly from image features instead of their second-order statistics, by analyzing the similarities and differences between multiple styles and considering the style distribution. Specifically, we present Contrastive Arbitrary Style Transfer (CAST), which is a new style representation learning and style transfer method via contrastive learning. Our framework consists of three key components, i.e., a multi-layer style projector for style code encoding, a domain enhancement module for effective learning of style distribution, and a generative network for image style transfer. We conduct qualitative and quantitative evaluations comprehensively to demonstrate that our approach achieves significantly better results compared to those obtained via state-of-the-art methods. Code and models are available at https://github.com/zyxElsa/CAST_pytorch
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们使用新颖的风格特征表示学习方法解决任意图像风格迁移的挑战性问题。合适的风格表示作为图像风格化任务的关键组成部分，对于获得满意的结果至关重要。现有的基于深度神经网络的方法在二阶统计（例如内容特征的格拉姆矩阵）的指导下取得了合理的结果。然而，它们没有利用足够的风格信息，从而导致局部扭曲和风格不一致等伪影。为了解决这些问题，我们建议通过分析多种风格之间的异同并考虑风格分布，直接从图像特征而不是二阶统计中学习风格表示。具体来说，我们提出了对比任意风格迁移（CAST），这是一种通过对比学习的新风格表示学习和风格迁移方法。我们的框架由三个关键组件组成，即用于风格代码编码的多层风格投影仪、用于有效学习风格分布的域增强模块以及用于图像风格迁移的生成网络。我们全面进行定性和定量评估，以证明我们的方法与通过最先进的方法获得的结果相比取得了明显更好的结果。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/zyxElsa/CAST_pytorch 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于对比学习的域增强任意图像风格迁移方法，通过改进风格表示学习解决现有方法的风格不一致和局部失真问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于深度学习的风格迁移方法依赖于内容特征的二阶统计量（如Gram矩阵），未能充分捕捉风格信息，导致局部失真和风格不一致等 artifacts。
AI.method: 提出对比任意风格迁移（CAST）框架，包含多层风格投影器（编码风格代码）、域增强模块（学习风格分布）和生成网络，通过对比学习直接从图像特征中学习风格表示。
AI.result: 在定性和定量评估中，CAST 相比现有方法显著提升了风格迁移效果，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 通过对比学习和域增强模块，CAST 有效解决了风格表示不足的问题，实现了更高质量的任意图像风格迁移。

title: Data-driven prediction of Air Traffic Controllers reactions to resolving conflicts
title_zh: 数据驱动预测空中交通管制员对解决冲突的反应
abstract: With the aim to enhance automation in conflict detection and resolution (CD&R) tasks in the Air Traffic Management domain, in this paper we propose deep learning techniques (DL) that can learn models of Air Traffic Controllers' (ATCO) reactions in resolving conflicts that can violate separation minimum constraints among aircraft trajectories: This implies learning when the ATCO will react towards resolving a conflict, and how he/she will react. Timely reactions, to which this paper aims, focus on when do reactions happen, aiming to predict the trajectory points, as the trajectory evolves, that the ATCO issues a conflict resolution action, while also predicting the type of resolution action (if any). Towards this goal, the paper formulates the ATCO reactions prediction problem for CD&R, and presents DL methods that can model ATCO timely reactions and evaluates these methods in real-world data sets, showing their efficacy in prediction with very high accuracy.
summary: With the aim to enhance automation in conflict detection and resolution (CD&R) tasks in the Air Traffic Management domain, in this paper we propose deep learning techniques (DL) that can learn models of Air Traffic Controllers' (ATCO) reactions in resolving conflicts that can violate separation minimum constraints among aircraft trajectories: This implies learning when the ATCO will react towards resolving a conflict, and how he/she will react. Timely reactions, to which this paper aims, focus on when do reactions happen, aiming to predict the trajectory points, as the trajectory evolves, that the ATCO issues a conflict resolution action, while also predicting the type of resolution action (if any). Towards this goal, the paper formulates the ATCO reactions prediction problem for CD&R, and presents DL methods that can model ATCO timely reactions and evaluates these methods in real-world data sets, showing their efficacy in prediction with very high accuracy.
abstract_zh: 为了增强空中交通管理领域冲突检测和解决（CD＆R）任务的自动化，在本文中，我们提出了深度学习技术（DL），该技术可以学习空中交通管制员（ATCO）在解决冲突时的反应模型，这些冲突可能违反飞机轨迹之间的最小间隔约束：这意味着学习ATCO何时会对解决冲突做出反应，以及他/她将如何反应。本文的目标是及时反应，重点关注反应何时发生，旨在预测随着轨迹的发展，ATCO 发出冲突解决行动的轨迹点，同时还预测解决行动的类型（如果有）。为了实现这一目标，本文提出了 CD&R 的 ATCO 反应预测问题，并提出了可以对 ATCO 及时反应进行建模的深度学习方法，并在现实数据集中评估这些方法，显示了它们在预测方面的有效性，具有非常高的准确度。

AI.tldr: 本论文旨在利用深度学习技术预测空中交通管制员在冲突检测与解决任务中的反应时机及类型，以提高自动化水平。
AI.motivation: 为提高空中交通管理中冲突检测与解决（CD&R）任务的自动化水平，需准确预测管制员的反应，以优化决策支持系统。
AI.method: 采用深度学习方法构建模型，学习管制员在冲突场景中的反应模式，并基于真实世界数据集进行验证。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，所提出的方法在预测管制员反应时机和类型方面具有很高的准确性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究验证了深度学习方法在预测管制员反应中的有效性，为未来自动化决策支持系统提供了理论依据。

search_text: Data-driven prediction of Air Traffic Controllers reactions to resolving conflicts 数据驱动预测空中交通管制员对解决冲突的反应 With the aim to enhance automation in conflict detection and resolution (CD&R) tasks in the Air Traffic Management domain, in this paper we propose deep learning techniques (DL) that can learn models of Air Traffic Controllers' (ATCO) reactions in resolving conflicts that can violate separation minimum constraints among aircraft trajectories: This implies learning when the ATCO will react towards resolving a conflict, and how he/she will react. Timely reactions, to which this paper aims, focus on when do reactions happen, aiming to predict the trajectory points, as the trajectory evolves, that the ATCO issues a conflict resolution action, while also predicting the type of resolution action (if any). Towards this goal, the paper formulates the ATCO reactions prediction problem for CD&R, and presents DL methods that can model ATCO timely reactions and evaluates these methods in real-world data sets, showing their efficacy in prediction with very high accuracy. 为了增强空中交通管理领域冲突检测和解决（CD＆R）任务的自动化，在本文中，我们提出了深度学习技术（DL），该技术可以学习空中交通管制员（ATCO）在解决冲突时的反应模型，这些冲突可能违反飞机轨迹之间的最小间隔约束：这意味着学习ATCO何时会对解决冲突做出反应，以及他/她将如何反应。本文的目标是及时反应，重点关注反应何时发生，旨在预测随着轨迹的发展，ATCO 发出冲突解决行动的轨迹点，同时还预测解决行动的类型（如果有）。为了实现这一目标，本文提出了 CD&R 的 ATCO 反应预测问题，并提出了可以对 ATCO 及时反应进行建模的深度学习方法，并在现实数据集中评估这些方法，显示了它们在预测方面的有效性，具有非常高的准确度。 本论文旨在利用深度学习技术预测空中交通管制员在冲突检测与解决任务中的反应时机及类型，以提高自动化水平。 Alevizos Bastas,George A. Vouros cs.AI,cs.LG

title: IFTT-PIN: A PIN-Entry Method Leveraging the Self-Calibration Paradigm
title_zh: IFTT-PIN：利用自校准范式的 PIN 输入方法
abstract: IFTT-PIN is a self-calibrating version of the PIN-entry method introduced in Roth et al. (2004) [1]. In [1], digits are split into two sets and assigned a color respectively. To communicate their digit, users press the button with the same color that is assigned to their digit, which can thus be identified by elimination after a few iterations. IFTT-PIN uses the same principle but does not pre-assign colors to each button. Instead, users are free to choose which button to use for each color. The button-to-color mapping only exists in the user's mind and is never directly communicated to the interface. In other words, IFTT-PIN infers both the user's PIN and their preferred button-to-color mapping at the same time, a process called self-calibration. In this paper, we present online interactive demonstrations of IFTT-PIN with and without self-calibration and introduce the key concepts and assumptions making self-calibration possible. IFTT-PIN can be tested at https://jgrizou.github.io/IFTT-PIN/ with a video introduction available at https://youtu.be/5I1ibPJdLHM. We review related work in the field of brain-computer interface and further propose self-calibration as a novel approach to protect users against shoulder surfing attacks. Finally, we introduce a vault cracking challenge as a test of usability and security that was informally tested at our institute. With IFTT-PIN, we wish to demonstrate a new interactive experience where users can decide actively and on-the-fly how to use an interface. The self-calibration paradigm might lead to novel opportunities for interaction in other applications or domains. We hope this work will inspire the community to invent them.

abstract_zh: IFTT-PIN 是 Roth 等人提出的 PIN 输入方法的自校准版本。 （2004）[1]。在[1]中，数字被分成两组并分别分配一种颜色。为了传达他们的数字，用户按下与分配给他们的数字相同颜色的按钮，因此可以在几次迭代后通过消除来识别。 IFTT-PIN 使用相同的原理，但没有为每个按钮预先分配颜色。相反，用户可以自由选择每种颜色使用哪个按钮。按钮到颜色的映射仅存在于用户的脑海中，并且永远不会直接传达到界面。换句话说，IFTT-PIN 同时推断用户的 PIN 及其首选按钮到颜色的映射，这一过程称为自校准。在本文中，我们展示了具有和不具有自校准功能的 IFTT-PIN 的在线交互式演示，并介绍了使自校准成为可能的关键概念和假设。 IFTT-PIN 可以在 https://jgrizou.github.io/IFTT-PIN/ 上进行测试，并在 https://youtu.be/5I1ibPJdLHM 上提供视频介绍。我们回顾了脑机接口领域的相关工作，并进一步提出自校准作为保护用户免受肩窥攻击的一种新方法。最后，我们引入了金库破解挑战，作为可用性和安全性的测试，并在我们的研究所进行了非正式测试。通过 IFTT-PIN，我们希望展示一种新的交互体验，用户可以主动、即时地决定如何使用界面。自校准范例可能会为其他应用程序或领域的交互带来新的机会。我们希望这项工作能够激励社区发明它们。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为IFTT-PIN的自校准PIN输入方法，通过用户自主选择按钮与颜色的映射关系，提升安全性并防止肩窥攻击。
AI.motivation: 传统PIN输入方法易受肩窥攻击，而预设颜色映射可能降低安全性。本文旨在通过自校准机制让用户动态决定颜色与按钮的对应关系，增强隐私保护。
AI.method: IFTT-PIN采用自校准范式，用户无需预设颜色与按钮的绑定，系统通过交互过程同时推断用户PIN码及其实时选择的按钮-颜色映射关系，并提供在线演示与安全测试挑战。
AI.result: 实现了IFTT-PIN的在线交互演示，通过非正式测试验证其可用性与安全性，并提出自校准范式在脑机接口等领域的潜在应用价值。
AI.conclusion: IFTT-PIN通过用户自主决策机制革新了交互体验，自校准范式为其他应用场景提供了新的设计思路，有望推动更安全的人机交互方式发展。

title: Estimating the ultrasound attenuation coefficient using convolutional neural networks -- a feasibility study
title_zh: 使用卷积神经网络估计超声衰减系数——可行性研究
abstract: Attenuation coefficient (AC) is a fundamental measure of tissue acoustical properties, which can be used in medical diagnostics. In this work, we investigate the feasibility of using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to directly estimate AC from radio-frequency (RF) ultrasound signals. To develop the CNNs we used RF signals collected from tissue mimicking numerical phantoms for the AC values in a range from 0.1 to 1.5 dB/(MHz*cm). The models were trained based on 1-D patches of RF data. We obtained mean absolute AC estimation errors of 0.08, 0.12, 0.20, 0.25 for the patch lengths: 10 mm, 5 mm, 2 mm and 1 mm, respectively. We explain the performance of the model by visualizing the frequency content associated with convolutional filters. Our study presents that the AC can be calculated using deep learning, and the weights of the CNNs can have physical interpretation.
summary: Attenuation coefficient (AC) is a fundamental measure of tissue acoustical properties, which can be used in medical diagnostics. In this work, we investigate the feasibility of using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to directly estimate AC from radio-frequency (RF) ultrasound signals. To develop the CNNs we used RF signals collected from tissue mimicking numerical phantoms for the AC values in a range from 0.1 to 1.5 dB/(MHz*cm). The models were trained based on 1-D patches of RF data. We obtained mean absolute AC estimation errors of 0.08, 0.12, 0.20, 0.25 for the patch lengths: 10 mm, 5 mm, 2 mm and 1 mm, respectively. We explain the performance of the model by visualizing the frequency content associated with convolutional filters. Our study presents that the AC can be calculated using deep learning, and the weights of the CNNs can have physical interpretation.
abstract_zh: 衰减系数 (AC) 是组织声学特性的基本度量，可用于医学诊断。在这项工作中，我们研究了使用卷积神经网络 (CNN) 直接从射频 (RF) 超声信号估计 AC 的可行性。为了开发 CNN，我们使用从组织模拟数字模型收集的 RF 信号，AC 值范围为 0.1 至 1.5 dB/(MHz*cm)。这些模型是根据一维射频数据块进行训练的。对于贴片长度：10 毫米、5 毫米、2 毫米和 1 毫米，我们获得的平均绝对 AC 估计误差分别为 0.08、0.12、0.20、0.25。我们通过可视化与卷积滤波器相关的频率内容来解释模型的性能。我们的研究表明，AC 可以使用深度学习来计算，并且 CNN 的权重可以具有物理解释。

AI.tldr: 本研究探讨了使用卷积神经网络（CNN）直接从射频（RF）超声信号中估计组织衰减系数（AC）的可行性。
AI.motivation: 衰减系数是评估组织声学特性的关键参数，传统测量方法存在局限性，而深度学习可能提供更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 基于数值模拟的组织仿体生成RF信号数据，使用1-D RF数据块训练CNN模型，并通过可视化卷积滤波器频率特征分析模型性能。
AI.result: 不同长度数据块的平均绝对误差分别为：10mm（0.08）、5mm（0.12）、2mm（0.20）、1mm（0.25）dB/(MHz·cm)。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习可有效估计超声衰减系数，且CNN权重具有物理可解释性，为医学诊断提供新方法。

title: Simple Regularisation for Uncertainty-Aware Knowledge Distillation
title_zh: 不确定性知识蒸馏的简单正则化
comment: Accepted to the ICML 2022 Workshop on Distribution-Free Uncertainty Quantification. The code can be found at: https://github.com/martinferianc/hydra_plus
comments: Accepted to the ICML 2022 Workshop on Distribution-Free Uncertainty Quantification. The code can be found at: https://github.com/martinferianc/hydra_plus
abstract: Considering uncertainty estimation of modern neural networks (NNs) is one of the most important steps towards deploying machine learning systems to meaningful real-world applications such as in medicine, finance or autonomous systems. At the moment, ensembles of different NNs constitute the state-of-the-art in both accuracy and uncertainty estimation in different tasks. However, ensembles of NNs are unpractical under real-world constraints, since their computation and memory consumption scale linearly with the size of the ensemble, which increase their latency and deployment cost. In this work, we examine a simple regularisation approach for distribution-free knowledge distillation of ensemble of machine learning models into a single NN. The aim of the regularisation is to preserve the diversity, accuracy and uncertainty estimation characteristics of the original ensemble without any intricacies, such as fine-tuning. We demonstrate the generality of the approach on combinations of toy data, SVHN/CIFAR-10, simple to complex NN architectures and different tasks.
summary: Considering uncertainty estimation of modern neural networks (NNs) is one of the most important steps towards deploying machine learning systems to meaningful real-world applications such as in medicine, finance or autonomous systems. At the moment, ensembles of different NNs constitute the state-of-the-art in both accuracy and uncertainty estimation in different tasks. However, ensembles of NNs are unpractical under real-world constraints, since their computation and memory consumption scale linearly with the size of the ensemble, which increase their latency and deployment cost. In this work, we examine a simple regularisation approach for distribution-free knowledge distillation of ensemble of machine learning models into a single NN. The aim of the regularisation is to preserve the diversity, accuracy and uncertainty estimation characteristics of the original ensemble without any intricacies, such as fine-tuning. We demonstrate the generality of the approach on combinations of toy data, SVHN/CIFAR-10, simple to complex NN architectures and different tasks.
abstract_zh: 考虑现代神经网络 (NN) 的不确定性估计是将机器学习系统部署到有意义的现实世界应用（例如医学、金融或自治系统）的最重要步骤之一。目前，不同神经网络的集成构成了不同任务中准确性和不确定性估计的最先进技术。然而，神经网络的集成在现实世界的限制下是不切实际的，因为它们的计算和内存消耗随着集成的大小线性扩展，这增加了它们的延迟和部署成本。在这项工作中，我们研究了一种简单的正则化方法，用于将机器学习模型集合的无分布知识蒸馏为单个神经网络。正则化的目的是保留原始集成的多样性、准确性和不确定性估计特性，而不需要任何复杂的操作，例如微调。我们展示了该方法在玩具数据、SVHN/CIFAR-10、简单到复杂的神经网络架构和不同任务组合上的通用性。

AI.tldr: 提出一种简单正则化方法，将集成模型的知识蒸馏到单个神经网络中，以保持其不确定性和准确性。
AI.motivation: 集成模型虽在准确性和不确定性估计上表现优异，但计算和内存消耗高，难以实际部署，需更高效方法。
AI.method: 采用简单正则化技术，在无需微调的情况下进行分布无关的知识蒸馏，保留集成模型的多样性与不确定性估计能力。
AI.result: 方法在玩具数据集、SVHN/CIFAR-10及多种网络架构和任务中均表现出良好的通用性和有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为实际应用提供了高效替代集成模型的解决方案，兼顾性能与部署效率。

title: Dynamic SAFFRON: Disease Control Over Time Via Group Testing
title_zh: 动态藏红花：通过小组测试随时间控制疾病
abstract: We consider the dynamic infection spread model that is based on the discrete SIR model which assumes infections to be spread over time via infected and non-isolated individuals. In our system, the main objective is not to minimize the number of required tests to identify every infection, but instead, to utilize the available, given testing capacity $T$ at each time instance to efficiently control the infection spread. We introduce and study a novel performance metric, which we coin as $ε$-disease control time. This metric can be used to measure how fast a given algorithm can control the spread of a disease. We characterize the performance of dynamic individual testing algorithm and introduce a novel dynamic SAFFRON based group testing algorithm. We present theoretical results and implement the proposed algorithms to compare their performances.
summary: We consider the dynamic infection spread model that is based on the discrete SIR model which assumes infections to be spread over time via infected and non-isolated individuals. In our system, the main objective is not to minimize the number of required tests to identify every infection, but instead, to utilize the available, given testing capacity $T$ at each time instance to efficiently control the infection spread. We introduce and study a novel performance metric, which we coin as $ε$-disease control time. This metric can be used to measure how fast a given algorithm can control the spread of a disease. We characterize the performance of dynamic individual testing algorithm and introduce a novel dynamic SAFFRON based group testing algorithm. We present theoretical results and implement the proposed algorithms to compare their performances.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑基于离散 SIR 模型的动态感染传播模型，该模型假设感染随着时间的推移通过感染者和非隔离个体传播。在我们的系统中，主要目标不是最大限度地减少识别每次感染所需的测试数量，而是利用每个时间实例的可用测试能力 $T$ 来有效控制感染传播。我们引入并研究了一种新颖的绩效指标，我们将其命名为$ε$-疾病控制时间。该指标可用于衡量给定算法控制疾病传播的速度。我们表征了动态个体测试算法的性能，并引入了一种新颖的基于 SAFFRON 的动态组测试算法。我们提出理论结果并实现所提出的算法来比较它们的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文主要研究在动态感染传播模型下，如何利用分组测试方法有效控制疾病传播，而非单纯减少测试次数。
AI.motivation: 传统方法侧重于最小化检测次数，但实际中需在有限测试资源下高效控制疫情，故提出新指标与算法。
AI.method: 引入ε-疾病控制时间指标，提出动态SAFFRON分组测试算法，并进行理论分析与实验验证。
AI.result: 实验显示动态SAFFRON算法在控制疾病传播速度上优于传统个体测试方法。
AI.conclusion: 动态SAFFRON算法能有效控制疫情，理论结果支持其性能优势。

authors_text: Aadhitiya VS,Jani Babu Shaik,Sonal Singhal,Siona Menezes Picardo,Nilesh Goel
title: Design and Mathematical Modelling of Inter Spike Interval of Temporal Neuromorphic Encoder for Image Recognition
title_zh: 用于图像识别的时间神经形态编码器的尖峰间间隔的设计和数学建模
comment: 4 pages, 6 figures, one table, IEEE ICEE 2020 conference proceeding
comments: 4 pages, 6 figures, one table, IEEE ICEE 2020 conference proceeding
abstract: Neuromorphic computing systems emulate the electrophysiological behavior of the biological nervous system using mixed-mode analog or digital VLSI circuits. These systems show superior accuracy and power efficiency in carrying out cognitive tasks. The neural network architecture used in neuromorphic computing systems is spiking neural networks (SNNs) analogous to the biological nervous system. SNN operates on spike trains as a function of time. A neuromorphic encoder converts sensory data into spike trains. In this paper, a low-power neuromorphic encoder for image processing is implemented. A mathematical model between pixels of an image and the inter-spike intervals is also formulated. Wherein an exponential relationship between pixels and inter-spike intervals is obtained. Finally, the mathematical equation is validated with circuit simulation.
summary: Neuromorphic computing systems emulate the electrophysiological behavior of the biological nervous system using mixed-mode analog or digital VLSI circuits. These systems show superior accuracy and power efficiency in carrying out cognitive tasks. The neural network architecture used in neuromorphic computing systems is spiking neural networks (SNNs) analogous to the biological nervous system. SNN operates on spike trains as a function of time. A neuromorphic encoder converts sensory data into spike trains. In this paper, a low-power neuromorphic encoder for image processing is implemented. A mathematical model between pixels of an image and the inter-spike intervals is also formulated. Wherein an exponential relationship between pixels and inter-spike intervals is obtained. Finally, the mathematical equation is validated with circuit simulation.
abstract_zh: 神经形态计算系统使用混合模式模拟或数字 VLSI 电路模拟生物神经系统的电生理行为。这些系统在执行认知任务时表现出卓越的准确性和功效。神经形态计算系统中使用的神经网络架构是类似于生物神经系统的尖峰神经网络（SNN）。 SNN 作为时间的函数在尖峰序列上运行。神经形态编码器将感觉数据转换为尖峰序列。在本文中，实现了一种用于图像处理的低功耗神经形态编码器。还制定了图像像素与尖峰间间隔之间的数学模型。其中获得像素与尖峰间间隔之间的指数关系。最后，通过电路仿真验证了数学方程。

AI.tldr: 本文设计并建立了用于图像识别的时序神经形态编码器的间隔间隔数学模型。
AI.motivation: 神经形态计算系统在执行认知任务时表现出更高的准确性和能效，但需要高效的编码器将感官数据转换为脉冲序列。
AI.method: 通过建立像素与脉冲间隔之间的指数关系模型，并利用电路仿真验证该数学模型。
AI.result: 成功实现了低功耗的图像处理神经形态编码器，并验证了像素与脉冲间隔之间的指数关系。
AI.conclusion: 该数学模型为神经形态计算系统中的图像识别任务提供了有效的编码方法，有助于提升系统的能效和性能。

Finally, the mathematical equation is validated with circuit simulation. 神经形态计算系统使用混合模式模拟或数字 VLSI 电路模拟生物神经系统的电生理行为。这些系统在执行认知任务时表现出卓越的准确性和功效。神经形态计算系统中使用的神经网络架构是类似于生物神经系统的尖峰神经网络（SNN）。 SNN 作为时间的函数在尖峰序列上运行。神经形态编码器将感觉数据转换为尖峰序列。在本文中，实现了一种用于图像处理的低功耗神经形态编码器。还制定了图像像素与尖峰间间隔之间的数学模型。其中获得像素与尖峰间间隔之间的指数关系。最后，通过电路仿真验证了数学方程。 本文设计并建立了用于图像识别的时序神经形态编码器的间隔间隔数学模型。 Aadhitiya VS,Jani Babu Shaik,Sonal Singhal,Siona Menezes Picardo,Nilesh Goel eess.SY,cs.NE

authors_text: Xiangyuan Yang,Jie Lin,Hanlin Zhang,Xinyu Yang,Peng Zhao
title: Gradient Aligned Attacks via a Few Queries
title_zh: 通过一些查询进行梯度对齐攻击
abstract: Black-box query attacks, which rely only on the output of the victim model, have proven to be effective in attacking deep learning models. However, existing black-box query attacks show low performance in a novel scenario where only a few queries are allowed. To address this issue, we propose gradient aligned attacks (GAA), which use the gradient aligned losses (GAL) we designed on the surrogate model to estimate the accurate gradient to improve the attack performance on the victim model. Specifically, we propose a gradient aligned mechanism to ensure that the derivatives of the loss function with respect to the logit vector have the same weight coefficients between the surrogate and victim models. Using this mechanism, we transform the cross-entropy (CE) loss and margin loss into gradient aligned forms, i.e. the gradient aligned CE or margin losses. These losses not only improve the attack performance of our gradient aligned attacks in the novel scenario but also increase the query efficiency of existing black-box query attacks. Through theoretical and empirical analysis on the ImageNet database, we demonstrate that our gradient aligned mechanism is effective, and that our gradient aligned attacks can improve the attack performance in the novel scenario by 16.1\% and 31.3\% on the $l_2$ and $l_{\infty}$ norms of the box constraint, respectively, compared to four latest transferable prior-based query attacks. Additionally, the gradient aligned losses also significantly reduce the number of queries required in these transferable prior-based query attacks by a maximum factor of 2.9 times. Overall, our proposed gradient aligned attacks and losses show significant improvements in the attack performance and query efficiency of black-box query attacks, particularly in scenarios where only a few queries are allowed.

However, existing black-box query attacks show low performance in a novel scenario where only a few queries are allowed. To address this issue, we propose gradient aligned attacks (GAA), which use the gradient aligned losses (GAL) we designed on the surrogate model to estimate the accurate gradient to improve the attack performance on the victim model. Specifically, we propose a gradient aligned mechanism to ensure that the derivatives of the loss function with respect to the logit vector have the same weight coefficients between the surrogate and victim models. Using this mechanism, we transform the cross-entropy (CE) loss and margin loss into gradient aligned forms, i.e. the gradient aligned CE or margin losses. These losses not only improve the attack performance of our gradient aligned attacks in the novel scenario but also increase the query efficiency of existing black-box query attacks. Through theoretical and empirical analysis on the ImageNet database, we demonstrate that our gradient aligned mechanism is effective, and that our gradient aligned attacks can improve the attack performance in the novel scenario by 16.1\% and 31.3\% on the $l_2$ and $l_{\infty}$ norms of the box constraint, respectively, compared to four latest transferable prior-based query attacks. Additionally, the gradient aligned losses also significantly reduce the number of queries required in these transferable prior-based query attacks by a maximum factor of 2.9 times. Overall, our proposed gradient aligned attacks and losses show significant improvements in the attack performance and query efficiency of black-box query attacks, particularly in scenarios where only a few queries are allowed.
abstract_zh: 黑盒查询攻击仅依赖于受害者模型的输出，已被证明可以有效地攻击深度学习模型。然而，现有的黑盒查询攻击在只允许少量查询的新场景中表现出较低的性能。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了梯度对齐攻击（GAA），它使用我们在代理模型上设计的梯度对齐损失（GAL）来估计准确的梯度，以提高对受害者模型的攻击性能。具体来说，我们提出了一种梯度对齐机制，以确保损失函数相对于 logit 向量的导数在代理模型和受害者模型之间具有相同的权重系数。利用这种机制，我们将交叉熵（CE）损失和边际损失转化为梯度对齐形式，即梯度对齐CE或边际损失。这些损失不仅提高了新场景中梯度对齐攻击的攻击性能，而且还提高了现有黑盒查询攻击的查询效率。通过对 ImageNet 数据库的理论和实证分析，我们证明了我们的梯度对齐机制是有效的，并且与四种最新的可转移基于先验的查询攻击相比，我们的梯度对齐攻击可以在盒约束的 $l_2$ 和 $l_{\infty}$ 规范上分别将新场景中的攻击性能提高 16.1% 和 31.3%。此外，梯度对齐损失还显着减少了这些可转移的基于先验的查询攻击所需的查询数量，最多减少了 2.9 倍。总体而言，我们提出的梯度对齐攻击和损失显示黑盒查询攻击的攻击性能和查询效率显着提高，特别是在只允许少量查询的情况下。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种梯度对齐攻击（GAA）方法，通过设计梯度对齐损失（GAL）提升黑盒查询攻击在少量查询场景下的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有黑盒查询攻击在仅允许少量查询的场景下表现不佳，亟需提升攻击效率与准确性。
AI.method: 通过构建梯度对齐机制，使代理模型与目标模型的损失函数梯度权重一致，并将交叉熵损失和边缘损失转化为梯度对齐形式。
AI.result: 在ImageNet数据集上，GAA在l2和l∞约束下分别提升攻击性能16.1%和31.3%，并将查询次数减少最多2.9倍。
AI.conclusion: 梯度对齐攻击及损失函数显著提升了黑盒攻击在低查询场景下的性能与效率，具有重要实践价值。

authors_text: Subrato Bharati,M. Rubaiyat Hossain Mondal,Prajoy Podder,V. B. Surya Prasath
title: Federated learning: Applications, challenges and future directions
title_zh: 联邦学习：应用、挑战和未来方向
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a system in which a central aggregator coordinates the efforts of multiple clients to solve machine learning problems. This setting allows training data to be dispersed in order to protect privacy. The purpose of this paper is to provide an overview of FL systems with a focus on healthcare. FL is evaluated here based on its frameworks, architectures, and applications. It is shown here that FL solves the preceding issues with a shared global deep learning (DL) model via a central aggregator server. This paper examines recent developments and provides a comprehensive list of unresolved issues, inspired by the rapid growth of FL research. In the context of FL, several privacy methods are described, including secure multiparty computation, homomorphic encryption, differential privacy, and stochastic gradient descent. Furthermore, a review of various FL classes, such as horizontal and vertical FL and federated transfer learning, is provided. FL has applications in wireless communication, service recommendation, intelligent medical diagnosis systems, and healthcare, all of which are discussed in this paper. We also present a thorough review of existing FL challenges, such as privacy protection, communication cost, system heterogeneity, and unreliable model upload, followed by future research directions.
summary: Federated learning (FL) is a system in which a central aggregator coordinates the efforts of multiple clients to solve machine learning problems. We also present a thorough review of existing FL challenges, such as privacy protection, communication cost, system heterogeneity, and unreliable model upload, followed by future research directions.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习（FL）是一个中央聚合器协调多个客户端的工作来解决机器学习问题的系统。此设置允许分散训练数据以保护隐私。本文的目的是概述 FL 系统，重点关注医疗保健。这里根据 FL 的框架、架构和应用程序对 FL 进行评估。此处显示，FL 通过中央聚合器服务器使用共享的全局深度学习 (DL) 模型解决了上述问题。本文受 FL 研究快速发展的启发，探讨了最新进展，并提供了未解决问题的完整列表。在 FL 的背景下，描述了几种隐私方法，包括安全多方计算、同态加密、差分隐私和随机梯度下降。此外，还提供了对各种 FL 类的回顾，例如水平和垂直 FL 以及联合迁移学习。 FL 在无线通信、服务推荐、智能医疗诊断系统和医疗保健方面都有应用，所有这些都将在本文中进行讨论。我们还对现有的 FL 挑战进行了彻底的回顾，例如隐私保护、通信成本、系统异构性和不可靠的模型上传，并提出了未来的研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了联邦学习（FL）在医疗健康领域的框架、架构、应用及挑战，分析其隐私保护方法与未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 传统机器学习面临数据隐私和分散性问题，FL通过分布式协作训练模型以保护隐私，尤其在医疗领域具有重要应用价值。
AI.method: 通过文献综述方法，系统梳理FL的隐私保护技术（如差分隐私、同态加密）、分类（横向/纵向FL、迁移学习）及在医疗诊断、通信等场景的应用，并总结现存挑战。
AI.result: 明确了FL在隐私保护、通信开销、系统异构性等方面的未解决问题，提出未来需优化算法效率、增强设备协同与保障模型可靠性等研究方向。
AI.conclusion: FL为隐私敏感场景提供了有效解决方案，但其在实际部署中仍面临多重技术挑战，需跨学科合作推动理论与应用突破。

We also present a thorough review of existing FL challenges, such as privacy protection, communication cost, system heterogeneity, and unreliable model upload, followed by future research directions. 联邦学习（FL）是一个中央聚合器协调多个客户端的工作来解决机器学习问题的系统。此设置允许分散训练数据以保护隐私。本文的目的是概述 FL 系统，重点关注医疗保健。这里根据 FL 的框架、架构和应用程序对 FL 进行评估。此处显示，FL 通过中央聚合器服务器使用共享的全局深度学习 (DL) 模型解决了上述问题。本文受 FL 研究快速发展的启发，探讨了最新进展，并提供了未解决问题的完整列表。在 FL 的背景下，描述了几种隐私方法，包括安全多方计算、同态加密、差分隐私和随机梯度下降。此外，还提供了对各种 FL 类的回顾，例如水平和垂直 FL 以及联合迁移学习。 FL 在无线通信、服务推荐、智能医疗诊断系统和医疗保健方面都有应用，所有这些都将在本文中进行讨论。我们还对现有的 FL 挑战进行了彻底的回顾，例如隐私保护、通信成本、系统异构性和不可靠的模型上传，并提出了未来的研究方向。 本文综述了联邦学习（FL）在医疗健康领域的框架、架构、应用及挑战，分析其隐私保护方法与未来研究方向。 Subrato Bharati,M. Rubaiyat Hossain Mondal,Prajoy Podder,V. B. Surya Prasath cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.IT,eess.IV

title: Minority Stress Experienced by LGBTQ Online Communities during the COVID-19 Pandemic
title_zh: COVID-19 大流行期间 LGBTQ 在线社区经历的少数群体压力
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has disproportionately impacted the lives of minorities, such as members of the LGBTQ community (lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer) due to pre-existing social disadvantages and health disparities. Although extensive research has been carried out on the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on different aspects of the general population's lives, few studies are focused on the LGBTQ population. In this paper, we develop and evaluate two sets of machine learning classifiers using a pre-pandemic and a during-pandemic dataset to identify Twitter posts exhibiting minority stress, which is a unique pressure faced by the members of the LGBTQ population due to their sexual and gender identities. We demonstrate that our best pre- and during-pandemic models show strong and stable performance for detecting posts that contain minority stress. We investigate the linguistic differences in minority stress posts across pre- and during-pandemic periods. We find that anger words are strongly associated with minority stress during the COVID-19 pandemic. We explore the impact of the pandemic on the emotional states of the LGBTQ population by adopting propensity score-based matching to perform a causal analysis. The results show that the LGBTQ population have a greater increase in the usage of cognitive words and worsened observable attribute in the usage of positive emotion words than the group of the general population with similar pre-pandemic behavioral attributes. Our findings have implications for the public health domain and policy-makers to provide adequate support, especially with respect to mental health, to the LGBTQ population during future crises.

We demonstrate that our best pre- and during-pandemic models show strong and stable performance for detecting posts that contain minority stress. We investigate the linguistic differences in minority stress posts across pre- and during-pandemic periods. We find that anger words are strongly associated with minority stress during the COVID-19 pandemic. We explore the impact of the pandemic on the emotional states of the LGBTQ population by adopting propensity score-based matching to perform a causal analysis. The results show that the LGBTQ population have a greater increase in the usage of cognitive words and worsened observable attribute in the usage of positive emotion words than the group of the general population with similar pre-pandemic behavioral attributes. Our findings have implications for the public health domain and policy-makers to provide adequate support, especially with respect to mental health, to the LGBTQ population during future crises.
abstract_zh: 由于先前存在的社会劣势和健康差异，COVID-19 大流行对少数群体的生活造成了不成比例的影响，例如 LGBTQ 社区成员（女同性恋、男同性恋、双性恋、跨性别者和酷儿）。尽管人们对 COVID-19 大流行对普通人群生活的不同方面的影响进行了广泛的研究，但很少有研究关注 LGBTQ 人群。在本文中，我们使用大流行前和大流行期间的数据集开发和评估了两组机器学习分类器，以识别表现出少数群体压力的 Twitter 帖子，这是 LGBTQ 群体成员因其性和性别身份而面临的独特压力。我们证明，我们最好的大流行前和大流行期间模型在检测包含少数群体压力的帖子方面表现出强大而稳定的性能。我们调查了大流行前和大流行期间少数族裔压力帖子的语言差异。我们发现，在 COVID-19 大流行期间，愤怒的言语与少数群体的压力密切相关。我们通过采用基于倾向得分的匹配来进行因果分析，探讨疫情对 LGBTQ 人群情绪状态的影响。结果表明，与大流行前具有相似行为属性的一般人群相比，LGBTQ 人群认知词汇的使用增加更多，而积极情绪词汇使用的可观察属性恶化。我们的研究结果对公共卫生领域和政策制定者在未来危机期间向 LGBTQ 群体提供足够的支持（尤其是在心理健康方面）具有重要意义。

AI.tldr: 本论文通过机器学习分析LGBTQ群体在疫情期间的社交媒体内容，研究其经历的少数群体压力变化。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情加剧了LGBTQ群体的健康不平等，但现有研究缺乏对其特定压力的系统分析，需探索疫情期间的情绪变化及影响因素。
AI.method: 构建疫情前及疫情期间的机器学习分类模型，识别Twitter中的少数群体压力内容，并采用倾向得分匹配进行因果分析。
AI.result: 模型准确识别压力内容，发现疫情期间愤怒词汇显著增加，LGBTQ群体认知词汇使用上升且积极情绪表达下降。
AI.conclusion: 研究结果提示需加强LGBTQ群体在公共卫生危机中的心理健康支持，政策制定应关注其特殊需求。

title: An Introduction to Quantum Machine Learning for Engineers
title_zh: 工程师量子机器学习简介
comment: The current version will appear on Foundations and Trends in Signal Processing
comments: The current version will appear on Foundations and Trends in Signal Processing
abstract: In the current noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) era, quantum machine learning is emerging as a dominant paradigm to program gate-based quantum computers. In quantum machine learning, the gates of a quantum circuit are parametrized, and the parameters are tuned via classical optimization based on data and on measurements of the outputs of the circuit. Parametrized quantum circuits (PQCs) can efficiently address combinatorial optimization problems, implement probabilistic generative models, and carry out inference (classification and regression). This monograph provides a self-contained introduction to quantum machine learning for an audience of engineers with a background in probability and linear algebra. It first describes the necessary background, concepts, and tools necessary to describe quantum operations and measurements. Then, it covers parametrized quantum circuits, the variational quantum eigensolver, as well as unsupervised and supervised quantum machine learning formulations.
summary: In the current noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) era, quantum machine learning is emerging as a dominant paradigm to program gate-based quantum computers. In quantum machine learning, the gates of a quantum circuit are parametrized, and the parameters are tuned via classical optimization based on data and on measurements of the outputs of the circuit. Parametrized quantum circuits (PQCs) can efficiently address combinatorial optimization problems, implement probabilistic generative models, and carry out inference (classification and regression). This monograph provides a self-contained introduction to quantum machine learning for an audience of engineers with a background in probability and linear algebra. It first describes the necessary background, concepts, and tools necessary to describe quantum operations and measurements. Then, it covers parametrized quantum circuits, the variational quantum eigensolver, as well as unsupervised and supervised quantum machine learning formulations.
abstract_zh: 在当前嘈杂的中尺度量子（NISQ）时代，量子机器学习正在成为基于门的量子计算机编程的主导范式。在量子机器学习中，量子电路的门被参数化，并通过基于数据和电路输出测量的经典优化来调整参数。参数化量子电路（PQC）可以有效地解决组合优化问题，实现概率生成模型，并进行推理（分类和回归）。本专着为具有概率和线性代数背景的工程师提供了量子机器学习的独立介绍。它首先描述了描述量子操作和测量所需的必要背景、概念和工具。然后，它涵盖了参数化量子电路、变分量子本征求解器以及无监督和监督量子机器学习公式。

AI.tldr: 本文为工程师介绍量子机器学习的基础概念与方法，重点阐述参数化量子电路及其在优化、生成模型和推理中的应用。
AI.motivation: 在当前NISQ时代，量子机器学习成为编程量子计算机的主流范式，需为具备概率与线性代数背景的工程师提供系统性入门指导。
AI.method: 通过讲解量子操作基础，系统介绍参数化量子电路、变分量子本征求解器及监督/无监督量子机器学习框架。
AI.result: 构建了面向工程师的量子机器学习知识体系，涵盖理论工具与实际应用案例，如组合优化与概率建模。
AI.conclusion: 本文为工程师提供了量子机器学习的实践路径，推动其在NISQ设备上的算法开发与工程应用。

authors_text: Michel Plüss,Manuela Hürlimann,Marc Cuny,Alla Stöckli,Nikolaos Kapotis,Julia Hartmann,Malgorzata Anna Ulasik,Christian Scheller,Yanick Schraner,Amit Jain,Jan Deriu,Mark Cieliebak,Manfred Vogel
title: SDS-200: A Swiss German Speech to Standard German Text Corpus
title_zh: SDS-200：标准德语文本语料库的瑞士德语语音
abstract: We present SDS-200, a corpus of Swiss German dialectal speech with Standard German text translations, annotated with dialect, age, and gender information of the speakers. The dataset allows for training speech translation, dialect recognition, and speech synthesis systems, among others. The data was collected using a web recording tool that is open to the public. Each participant was given a text in Standard German and asked to translate it to their Swiss German dialect before recording it. To increase the corpus quality, recordings were validated by other participants. The data consists of 200 hours of speech by around 4000 different speakers and covers a large part of the Swiss-German dialect landscape. We release SDS-200 alongside a baseline speech translation model, which achieves a word error rate (WER) of 30.3 and a BLEU score of 53.1 on the SDS-200 test set. Furthermore, we use SDS-200 to fine-tune a pre-trained XLS-R model, achieving 21.6 WER and 64.0 BLEU.
summary: We present SDS-200, a corpus of Swiss German dialectal speech with Standard German text translations, annotated with dialect, age, and gender information of the speakers. The dataset allows for training speech translation, dialect recognition, and speech synthesis systems, among others. The data was collected using a web recording tool that is open to the public. Each participant was given a text in Standard German and asked to translate it to their Swiss German dialect before recording it. To increase the corpus quality, recordings were validated by other participants. The data consists of 200 hours of speech by around 4000 different speakers and covers a large part of the Swiss-German dialect landscape. We release SDS-200 alongside a baseline speech translation model, which achieves a word error rate (WER) of 30.3 and a BLEU score of 53.1 on the SDS-200 test set. Furthermore, we use SDS-200 to fine-tune a pre-trained XLS-R model, achieving 21.6 WER and 64.0 BLEU.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 SDS-200，这是一个包含标准德语文本翻译的瑞士德语方言语音语料库，标注了说话者的方言、年龄和性别信息。该数据集可用于训练语音翻译、方言识别和语音合成系统等。这些数据是使用向公众开放的网络记录工具收集的。每位参与者都会收到一份标准德语文本，并要求在录制之前将其翻译成瑞士德语方言。为了提高语料库质量，其他参与者对录音进行了验证。该数据包含约 4000 名不同说话者 200 小时的讲话，涵盖了瑞士德语方言的大部分内容。我们发布了 SDS-200 以及基线语音翻译模型，该模型在 SDS-200 测试集上实现了 30.3 的单词错误率 (WER) 和 53.1 的 BLEU 分数。此外，我们使用 SDS-200 来微调预训练的 XLS-R 模型，实现 21.6 WER 和 64.0 BLEU。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了SDS-200语料库，包含瑞士德语方言语音及标准德语文本翻译，用于训练语音翻译、方言识别等系统。
AI.motivation: 现有资源缺乏覆盖瑞士德语方言的高质量语音与文本对齐数据，限制了相关模型的训练与研究。
AI.method: 通过公开网络工具收集4000名参与者语音数据，每人朗读标准德语文本并转为方言语音，经多人验证确保质量。
AI.result: 基线语音翻译模型在测试集上达到30.3 WER和53.1 BLEU；微调XLS-R模型后性能提升至21.6 WER和64.0 BLEU。
AI.conclusion: SDS-200为瑞士德语方言研究提供了大规模标注数据，可推动语音翻译、方言识别等任务的发展。

search_text: SDS-200: A Swiss German Speech to Standard German Text Corpus SDS-200：标准德语文本语料库的瑞士德语语音 We present SDS-200, a corpus of Swiss German dialectal speech with Standard German text translations, annotated with dialect, age, and gender information of the speakers. The dataset allows for training speech translation, dialect recognition, and speech synthesis systems, among others. The data was collected using a web recording tool that is open to the public. Each participant was given a text in Standard German and asked to translate it to their Swiss German dialect before recording it. To increase the corpus quality, recordings were validated by other participants. The data consists of 200 hours of speech by around 4000 different speakers and covers a large part of the Swiss-German dialect landscape. We release SDS-200 alongside a baseline speech translation model, which achieves a word error rate (WER) of 30.3 and a BLEU score of 53.1 on the SDS-200 test set. Furthermore, we use SDS-200 to fine-tune a pre-trained XLS-R model, achieving 21.6 WER and 64.0 BLEU. 我们提出了 SDS-200，这是一个包含标准德语文本翻译的瑞士德语方言语音语料库，标注了说话者的方言、年龄和性别信息。该数据集可用于训练语音翻译、方言识别和语音合成系统等。这些数据是使用向公众开放的网络记录工具收集的。每位参与者都会收到一份标准德语文本，并要求在录制之前将其翻译成瑞士德语方言。为了提高语料库质量，其他参与者对录音进行了验证。该数据包含约 4000 名不同说话者 200 小时的讲话，涵盖了瑞士德语方言的大部分内容。我们发布了 SDS-200 以及基线语音翻译模型，该模型在 SDS-200 测试集上实现了 30.3 的单词错误率 (WER) 和 53.1 的 BLEU 分数。此外，我们使用 SDS-200 来微调预训练的 XLS-R 模型，实现 21.6 WER 和 64.0 BLEU。 本文介绍了SDS-200语料库，包含瑞士德语方言语音及标准德语文本翻译，用于训练语音翻译、方言识别等系统。 Michel Plüss,Manuela Hürlimann,Marc Cuny,Alla Stöckli,Nikolaos Kapotis,Julia Hartmann,Malgorzata Anna Ulasik,Christian Scheller,Yanick Schraner,Amit Jain,Jan Deriu,Mark Cieliebak,Manfred Vogel cs.CL,cs.AI

title: Learning Feature Fusion for Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Person Re-identification
title_zh: 用于无监督域自适应行人重新识别的学习特征融合
abstract: Unsupervised domain adaptive (UDA) person re-identification (ReID) has gained increasing attention for its effectiveness on the target domain without manual annotations. Most fine-tuning based UDA person ReID methods focus on encoding global features for pseudo labels generation, neglecting the local feature that can provide for the fine-grained information. To handle this issue, we propose a Learning Feature Fusion (LF2) framework for adaptively learning to fuse global and local features to obtain a more comprehensive fusion feature representation. Specifically, we first pre-train our model within a source domain, then fine-tune the model on unlabeled target domain based on the teacher-student training strategy. The average weighting teacher network is designed to encode global features, while the student network updating at each iteration is responsible for fine-grained local features. By fusing these multi-view features, multi-level clustering is adopted to generate diverse pseudo labels. In particular, a learnable Fusion Module (FM) for giving prominence to fine-grained local information within the global feature is also proposed to avoid obscure learning of multiple pseudo labels. Experiments show that our proposed LF2 framework outperforms the state-of-the-art with 73.5% mAP and 83.7% Rank1 on Market1501 to DukeMTMC-ReID, and achieves 83.2% mAP and 92.8% Rank1 on DukeMTMC-ReID to Market1501.
summary: Unsupervised domain adaptive (UDA) person re-identification (ReID) has gained increasing attention for its effectiveness on the target domain without manual annotations. Most fine-tuning based UDA person ReID methods focus on encoding global features for pseudo labels generation, neglecting the local feature that can provide for the fine-grained information. To handle this issue, we propose a Learning Feature Fusion (LF2) framework for adaptively learning to fuse global and local features to obtain a more comprehensive fusion feature representation. Specifically, we first pre-train our model within a source domain, then fine-tune the model on unlabeled target domain based on the teacher-student training strategy. The average weighting teacher network is designed to encode global features, while the student network updating at each iteration is responsible for fine-grained local features. By fusing these multi-view features, multi-level clustering is adopted to generate diverse pseudo labels. In particular, a learnable Fusion Module (FM) for giving prominence to fine-grained local information within the global feature is also proposed to avoid obscure learning of multiple pseudo labels. Experiments show that our proposed LF2 framework outperforms the state-of-the-art with 73.5% mAP and 83.7% Rank1 on Market1501 to DukeMTMC-ReID, and achieves 83.2% mAP and 92.8% Rank1 on DukeMTMC-ReID to Market1501.
abstract_zh: 无监督域自适应 (UDA) 行人重新识别 (ReID) 因其在无需手动注释的情况下在目标域上的有效性而受到越来越多的关注。大多数基于微调的 UDA 行人 ReID 方法侧重于编码全局特征以生成伪标签，而忽略了可以提供细粒度信息的局部特征。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种学习特征融合（LF2）框架，用于自适应学习融合全局和局部特征，以获得更全面的融合特征表示。具体来说，我们首先在源域中预训练模型，然后根据师生训练策略在未标记的目标域上微调模型。平均加权教师网络旨在编码全局特征，而学生网络在每次迭代时更新负责细粒度的局部特征。通过融合这些多视图特征，采用多级聚类来生成不同的伪标签。特别是，还提出了一种可学习的融合模块（FM），用于突出全局特征中的细粒度局部信息，以避免多个伪标签的模糊学习。实验表明，我们提出的 LF2 框架在 Market1501 到 DukeMTMC-ReID 上的性能优于现有技术，达到 73.5% mAP 和 83.7% Rank1，并在 DukeMTMC-ReID 到 Market1501 上实现 83.2% mAP 和 92.8% Rank1。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LF2的框架，通过融合全局和局部特征来提升无监督域适应性行人重识别（ReID）的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有无监督域适应方法主要关注全局特征生成伪标签，忽略了局部特征提供的细粒度信息，导致模型表现不足。
AI.method: 采用教师-学生训练策略，教师网络编码全局特征，学生网络学习局部特征，并通过可学习的融合模块（FM）整合多视角特征，结合多级聚类生成多样化伪标签。
AI.result: 在Market1501到DukeMTMC-ReID数据集上达到73.5% mAP和83.7% Rank1，在反向任务中达到83.2% mAP和92.8% Rank1，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: LF2框架通过有效融合全局与局部特征，显著提升了跨域行人重识别的准确性，验证了细粒度信息对域适应的重要性。

ai_content.conclusion: LF2框架通过有效融合全局与局部特征，显著提升了跨域行人重识别的准确性，验证了细粒度信息对域适应的重要性。

authors_text: Jiayi Zheng,Ling Yang,Heyuan Wang,Cheng Yang,Yinghong Li,Xiaowei Hu,Shenda Hong
title: Spatial Autoregressive Coding for Graph Neural Recommendation
title_zh: 图神经推荐的空间自回归编码
abstract: Graph embedding methods including traditional shallow models and deep Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have led to promising applications in recommendation. Nevertheless, shallow models especially random-walk-based algorithms fail to adequately exploit neighbor proximity in sampled subgraphs or sequences due to their optimization paradigm. GNN-based algorithms suffer from the insufficient utilization of high-order information and easily cause over-smoothing problems when stacking too much layers, which may deteriorate the recommendations of low-degree (long-tail) items, limiting the expressiveness and scalability. In this paper, we propose a novel framework SAC, namely Spatial Autoregressive Coding, to solve the above problems in a unified way. To adequately leverage neighbor proximity and high-order information, we design a novel spatial autoregressive paradigm. Specifically, we first randomly mask multi-hop neighbors and embed the target node by integrating all other surrounding neighbors with an explicit multi-hop attention. Then we reinforce the model to learn a neighbor-predictive coding for the target node by contrasting the coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, equipped with a new hard negative sampling strategy. To learn the minimal sufficient representation for the target-to-neighbor prediction task and remove the redundancy of neighbors, we devise Neighbor Information Bottleneck by maximizing the mutual information between target predictive coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, and simultaneously constraining those between the coding and surrounding neighbors' embedding. Experimental results on both public recommendation datasets and a real scenario web-scale dataset Douyin-Friend-Recommendation demonstrate the superiority of SAC compared with state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Graph embedding methods including traditional shallow models and deep Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have led to promising applications in recommendation. Nevertheless, shallow models especially random-walk-based algorithms fail to adequately exploit neighbor proximity in sampled subgraphs or sequences due to their optimization paradigm. GNN-based algorithms suffer from the insufficient utilization of high-order information and easily cause over-smoothing problems when stacking too much layers, which may deteriorate the recommendations of low-degree (long-tail) items, limiting the expressiveness and scalability. In this paper, we propose a novel framework SAC, namely Spatial Autoregressive Coding, to solve the above problems in a unified way. To adequately leverage neighbor proximity and high-order information, we design a novel spatial autoregressive paradigm. Specifically, we first randomly mask multi-hop neighbors and embed the target node by integrating all other surrounding neighbors with an explicit multi-hop attention. Then we reinforce the model to learn a neighbor-predictive coding for the target node by contrasting the coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, equipped with a new hard negative sampling strategy. To learn the minimal sufficient representation for the target-to-neighbor prediction task and remove the redundancy of neighbors, we devise Neighbor Information Bottleneck by maximizing the mutual information between target predictive coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, and simultaneously constraining those between the coding and surrounding neighbors' embedding. Experimental results on both public recommendation datasets and a real scenario web-scale dataset Douyin-Friend-Recommendation demonstrate the superiority of SAC compared with state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 包括传统浅层模型和深度图神经网络（GNN）在内的图嵌入方法在推荐领域带来了有前景的应用。然而，浅层模型，尤其是基于随机游走的算法，由于其优化范式而无法充分利用采样子图或序列中的邻近邻近度。基于GNN的算法存在对高阶信息利用不足的问题，并且在堆叠太多层时容易导致过度平滑问题，这可能会恶化低度（长尾）项目的推荐，限制表达性和可扩展性。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的框架SAC，即空间自回归编码，以统一的方式解决上述问题。为了充分利用邻居邻近度和高阶信息，我们设计了一种新颖的空间自回归范式。具体来说，我们首先随机屏蔽多跳邻居，并通过将所有其他周围邻居与显式多跳注意力集成来嵌入目标节点。然后，我们通过对比编码和屏蔽邻居的嵌入来强化模型，以学习目标节点的邻居预测编码，并配备新的硬负采样策略。为了学习目标到邻居预测任务的最小充分表示并消除邻居的冗余，我们通过最大化目标预测编码和屏蔽邻居嵌入之间的互信息，并同时约束编码和周围邻居嵌入之间的互信息来设计邻居信息瓶颈。在公共推荐数据集和真实场景网络规模数据集 Douyin-Friend-Recommendation 上的实验结果证明了 SAC 与最先进方法相比的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为SAC的空间自回归编码框架，旨在解决图神经网络在推荐系统中高阶信息利用不足和过平滑问题。
AI.motivation: 传统浅层图模型无法充分捕捉邻居接近性，而深度GNN存在高阶信息利用不足和过平滑问题，导致长尾项目推荐效果受限。
AI.method: 设计空间自回归范式：1）随机掩码多跳邻居并结合多跳注意力编码目标节点；2）通过对比学习和硬负采样强化邻居预测；3）引入邻居信息瓶颈最大化目标编码与掩码邻居的互信息并约束冗余。
AI.result: 在公开数据集和抖音千万级真实场景数据集上均优于现有最先进方法，尤其提升长尾项目推荐效果。
AI.conclusion: SAC通过统一框架解决了图神经推荐中的关键问题，为高效建模高阶邻居关系和避免过平滑提供了新思路。

search_text: Spatial Autoregressive Coding for Graph Neural Recommendation 图神经推荐的空间自回归编码 Graph embedding methods including traditional shallow models and deep Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have led to promising applications in recommendation. Nevertheless, shallow models especially random-walk-based algorithms fail to adequately exploit neighbor proximity in sampled subgraphs or sequences due to their optimization paradigm. GNN-based algorithms suffer from the insufficient utilization of high-order information and easily cause over-smoothing problems when stacking too much layers, which may deteriorate the recommendations of low-degree (long-tail) items, limiting the expressiveness and scalability. In this paper, we propose a novel framework SAC, namely Spatial Autoregressive Coding, to solve the above problems in a unified way. To adequately leverage neighbor proximity and high-order information, we design a novel spatial autoregressive paradigm. Specifically, we first randomly mask multi-hop neighbors and embed the target node by integrating all other surrounding neighbors with an explicit multi-hop attention. Then we reinforce the model to learn a neighbor-predictive coding for the target node by contrasting the coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, equipped with a new hard negative sampling strategy. To learn the minimal sufficient representation for the target-to-neighbor prediction task and remove the redundancy of neighbors, we devise Neighbor Information Bottleneck by maximizing the mutual information between target predictive coding and the masked neighbors' embedding, and simultaneously constraining those between the coding and surrounding neighbors' embedding. Experimental results on both public recommendation datasets and a real scenario web-scale dataset Douyin-Friend-Recommendation demonstrate the superiority of SAC compared with state-of-the-art methods. 包括传统浅层模型和深度图神经网络（GNN）在内的图嵌入方法在推荐领域带来了有前景的应用。然而，浅层模型，尤其是基于随机游走的算法，由于其优化范式而无法充分利用采样子图或序列中的邻近邻近度。基于GNN的算法存在对高阶信息利用不足的问题，并且在堆叠太多层时容易导致过度平滑问题，这可能会恶化低度（长尾）项目的推荐，限制表达性和可扩展性。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的框架SAC，即空间自回归编码，以统一的方式解决上述问题。为了充分利用邻居邻近度和高阶信息，我们设计了一种新颖的空间自回归范式。具体来说，我们首先随机屏蔽多跳邻居，并通过将所有其他周围邻居与显式多跳注意力集成来嵌入目标节点。然后，我们通过对比编码和屏蔽邻居的嵌入来强化模型，以学习目标节点的邻居预测编码，并配备新的硬负采样策略。为了学习目标到邻居预测任务的最小充分表示并消除邻居的冗余，我们通过最大化目标预测编码和屏蔽邻居嵌入之间的互信息，并同时约束编码和周围邻居嵌入之间的互信息来设计邻居信息瓶颈。在公共推荐数据集和真实场景网络规模数据集 Douyin-Friend-Recommendation 上的实验结果证明了 SAC 与最先进方法相比的优越性。 本文提出一种名为SAC的空间自回归编码框架，旨在解决图神经网络在推荐系统中高阶信息利用不足和过平滑问题。 Jiayi Zheng,Ling Yang,Heyuan Wang,Cheng Yang,Yinghong Li,Xiaowei Hu,Shenda Hong cs.IR,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Yawen Zhao,Mingzhe Zhang,Chenhao Zhang,Weitong Chen,Nan Ye,Miao Xu
title: A Boosting Algorithm for Positive-Unlabeled Learning
title_zh: 一种用于正向无标记学习的增强算法
abstract: Positive-unlabeled (PU) learning deals with binary classification problems when only positive (P) and unlabeled (U) data are available. Many recent PU methods are based on neural networks, but little has been done to develop boosting algorithms for PU learning, despite boosting algorithms' strong performance on many fully supervised classification problems. In this paper, we propose a novel boosting algorithm, AdaPU, for PU learning. Similarly to AdaBoost, AdaPU aims to optimize an empirical exponential loss, but the loss is based on the PU data, rather than on positive-negative (PN) data. As in AdaBoost, we learn a weighted combination of weak classifiers by learning one weak classifier and its weight at a time. However, AdaPU requires a very different algorithm for learning the weak classifiers and determining their weights. This is because AdaPU learns a weak classifier and its weight using a weighted positive-negative (PN) dataset with some negative data weights $-$ the dataset is derived from the original PU data, and the data weights are determined by the current weighted classifier combination, but some data weights are negative. Our experiments showed that AdaPU outperforms neural networks on several benchmark PU datasets, including a large-scale challenging cyber security dataset.
summary: Positive-unlabeled (PU) learning deals with binary classification problems when only positive (P) and unlabeled (U) data are available. Many recent PU methods are based on neural networks, but little has been done to develop boosting algorithms for PU learning, despite boosting algorithms' strong performance on many fully supervised classification problems. In this paper, we propose a novel boosting algorithm, AdaPU, for PU learning. Similarly to AdaBoost, AdaPU aims to optimize an empirical exponential loss, but the loss is based on the PU data, rather than on positive-negative (PN) data. As in AdaBoost, we learn a weighted combination of weak classifiers by learning one weak classifier and its weight at a time. However, AdaPU requires a very different algorithm for learning the weak classifiers and determining their weights. This is because AdaPU learns a weak classifier and its weight using a weighted positive-negative (PN) dataset with some negative data weights $-$ the dataset is derived from the original PU data, and the data weights are determined by the current weighted classifier combination, but some data weights are negative. Our experiments showed that AdaPU outperforms neural networks on several benchmark PU datasets, including a large-scale challenging cyber security dataset.
abstract_zh: 当只有正 (P) 和未标记 (U) 数据可用时，正未标记 (PU) 学习可处理二元分类问题。最近的许多 PU 方法都是基于神经网络的，但尽管 boosting 算法在许多完全监督的分类问题上表现出色，但在开发用于 PU 学习的 boosting 算法方面却做得很少。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于 PU 学习的新型 boosting 算法 AdaPU。与 AdaBoost 类似，AdaPU 旨在优化经验指数损失，但损失是基于 PU 数据，而不是基于正负（PN）数据。与 AdaBoost 一样，我们通过一次学习一个弱分类器及其权重来学习弱分类器的加权组合。然而，AdaPU 需要一种非常不同的算法来学习弱分类器并确定它们的权重。这是因为 AdaPU 使用带有一些负数据权重的加权正负 (PN) 数据集来学习弱分类器及其权重 $-$ 该数据集源自原始 PU 数据，数据权重由当前加权分类器组合确定，但有些数据权重是负数。我们的实验表明，AdaPU 在多个基准 PU 数据集（包括具有挑战性的大规模网络安全数据集）上优于神经网络。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为AdaPU的 boosting 算法，用于解决仅包含正样本和未标记样本的二分类问题（PU学习）。
AI.motivation: 尽管 boosting 算法在全监督分类中表现优异，但现有 PU 学习方法多依赖神经网络，缺乏针对 PU 学习的 boosting 算法研究。
AI.method: AdaPU 通过优化基于 PU 数据的指数损失函数，迭代学习弱分类器及其权重。其核心是构建一个带负权重的加权正负样本集（从原始 PU 数据派生），并利用当前分类器组合动态调整数据权重。
AI.result: 实验表明，AdaPU 在多个基准 PU 数据集（包括大规模网络安全数据集）上优于神经网络方法。
AI.conclusion: AdaPU 为 PU 学习提供了有效的 boosting 框架，证明了在 PU 数据下通过引入负权重样本集可提升分类性能。

authors_text: Yang Xiang,Zhihua Wu,Weibao Gong,Siyu Ding,Xianjie Mo,Yuang Liu,Shuohuan Wang,Peng Liu,Yongshuai Hou,Long Li,Bin Wang,Shaohuai Shi,Yaqian Han,Yue Yu,Ge Li,Yu Sun,Yanjun Ma,Dianhai Yu
title: Nebula-I: A General Framework for Collaboratively Training Deep Learning Models on Low-Bandwidth Cloud Clusters
title_zh: Nebula-I：在低带宽云集群上协作训练深度学习模型的通用框架
abstract: The ever-growing model size and scale of compute have attracted increasing interests in training deep learning models over multiple nodes. However, when it comes to training on cloud clusters, especially across remote clusters, huge challenges are faced. In this work, we introduce a general framework, Nebula-I, for collaboratively training deep learning models over remote heterogeneous clusters, the connections between which are low-bandwidth wide area networks (WANs). We took natural language processing (NLP) as an example to show how Nebula-I works in different training phases that include: a) pre-training a multilingual language model using two remote clusters; and b) fine-tuning a machine translation model using knowledge distilled from pre-trained models, which run through the most popular paradigm of recent deep learning. To balance the accuracy and communication efficiency, in Nebula-I, parameter-efficient training strategies, hybrid parallel computing methods and adaptive communication acceleration techniques are jointly applied. Meanwhile, security strategies are employed to guarantee the safety, reliability and privacy in intra-cluster computation and inter-cluster communication. Nebula-I is implemented with the PaddlePaddle deep learning framework, which can support collaborative training over heterogeneous hardware, e.g. GPU and NPU. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework could substantially maximize the training efficiency while preserving satisfactory NLP performance. By using Nebula-I, users can run large-scale training tasks over cloud clusters with minimum developments, and the utility of existed large pre-trained models could be further promoted. We also introduced new state-of-the-art results on cross-lingual natural language inference tasks, which are generated based upon a novel learning framework and Nebula-I.
summary: The ever-growing model size and scale of compute have attracted increasing interests in training deep learning models over multiple nodes. However, when it comes to training on cloud clusters, especially across remote clusters, huge challenges are faced. In this work, we introduce a general framework, Nebula-I, for collaboratively training deep learning models over remote heterogeneous clusters, the connections between which are low-bandwidth wide area networks (WANs). To balance the accuracy and communication efficiency, in Nebula-I, parameter-efficient training strategies, hybrid parallel computing methods and adaptive communication acceleration techniques are jointly applied. Meanwhile, security strategies are employed to guarantee the safety, reliability and privacy in intra-cluster computation and inter-cluster communication. Nebula-I is implemented with the PaddlePaddle deep learning framework, which can support collaborative training over heterogeneous hardware, e.g. GPU and NPU. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework could substantially maximize the training efficiency while preserving satisfactory NLP performance. By using Nebula-I, users can run large-scale training tasks over cloud clusters with minimum developments, and the utility of existed large pre-trained models could be further promoted. We also introduced new state-of-the-art results on cross-lingual natural language inference tasks, which are generated based upon a novel learning framework and Nebula-I.
abstract_zh: 不断增长的模型大小和计算规模引起了人们对在多个节点上训练深度学习模型越来越感兴趣。然而，当涉及到云集群训练，尤其是跨远程集群的训练时，面临着巨大的挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一个通用框架 Nebula-I，用于在远程异构集群上协作训练深度学习模型，集群之间的连接是低带宽广域网 (WAN)。我们以自然语言处理（NLP）为例，展示 Nebula-I 在不同训练阶段的工作原理，包括：a）使用两个远程集群预训练多语言语言模型； b) 使用从预训练模型中提取的知识来微调机器翻译模型，这些模型贯穿了最近深度学习最流行的范式。为了平衡准确性和通信效率，Nebula-I联合应用了参数高效的训练策略、混合并行计算方法和自适应通信加速技术。同时，采用安全策略保证集群内计算和集群间通信的安全性、可靠性和隐私性。 Nebula-I采用PaddlePaddle深度学习框架实现，可以支持异构硬件上的协作训练，例如： GPU 和 NPU。实验表明，所提出的框架可以最大限度地提高训练效率，同时保持令人满意的 NLP 性能。通过使用Nebula-I，用户可以以最少的开发在云集群上运行大规模的训练任务，并进一步提升现有大型预训练模型的效用。我们还介绍了跨语言自然语言推理任务的最新成果，这些成果是基于新颖的学习框架和 Nebula-I 生成的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Nebula-I框架，用于在低带宽云集群上协作训练深度学习模型，解决跨集群训练中的通信效率与安全性问题。
AI.motivation: 随着模型规模扩大，跨远程异构集群训练面临带宽限制与安全挑战，需平衡训练效率、模型性能及隐私保护。
AI.method: 结合参数高效训练策略、混合并行计算、自适应通信加速技术及安全机制，基于PaddlePaddle框架实现异构硬件支持。
AI.result: 实验表明框架显著提升训练效率并保持NLP性能，且在跨语言自然推理任务中取得新SOTA结果。
AI.conclusion: Nebula-I有效解决低带宽集群协作训练难题，推动大规模预训练模型的实用化与跨语言任务性能突破。

search_text: Nebula-I: A General Framework for Collaboratively Training Deep Learning Models on Low-Bandwidth Cloud Clusters Nebula-I：在低带宽云集群上协作训练深度学习模型的通用框架 The ever-growing model size and scale of compute have attracted increasing interests in training deep learning models over multiple nodes. However, when it comes to training on cloud clusters, especially across remote clusters, huge challenges are faced. In this work, we introduce a general framework, Nebula-I, for collaboratively training deep learning models over remote heterogeneous clusters, the connections between which are low-bandwidth wide area networks (WANs). We took natural language processing (NLP) as an example to show how Nebula-I works in different training phases that include: a) pre-training a multilingual language model using two remote clusters; and b) fine-tuning a machine translation model using knowledge distilled from pre-trained models, which run through the most popular paradigm of recent deep learning. To balance the accuracy and communication efficiency, in Nebula-I, parameter-efficient training strategies, hybrid parallel computing methods and adaptive communication acceleration techniques are jointly applied. Meanwhile, security strategies are employed to guarantee the safety, reliability and privacy in intra-cluster computation and inter-cluster communication. Nebula-I is implemented with the PaddlePaddle deep learning framework, which can support collaborative training over heterogeneous hardware, e.g. GPU and NPU. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework could substantially maximize the training efficiency while preserving satisfactory NLP performance. By using Nebula-I, users can run large-scale training tasks over cloud clusters with minimum developments, and the utility of existed large pre-trained models could be further promoted. We also introduced new state-of-the-art results on cross-lingual natural language inference tasks, which are generated based upon a novel learning framework and Nebula-I. 不断增长的模型大小和计算规模引起了人们对在多个节点上训练深度学习模型越来越感兴趣。然而，当涉及到云集群训练，尤其是跨远程集群的训练时，面临着巨大的挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一个通用框架 Nebula-I，用于在远程异构集群上协作训练深度学习模型，集群之间的连接是低带宽广域网 (WAN)。我们以自然语言处理（NLP）为例，展示 Nebula-I 在不同训练阶段的工作原理，包括：a）使用两个远程集群预训练多语言语言模型； b) 使用从预训练模型中提取的知识来微调机器翻译模型，这些模型贯穿了最近深度学习最流行的范式。为了平衡准确性和通信效率，Nebula-I联合应用了参数高效的训练策略、混合并行计算方法和自适应通信加速技术。同时，采用安全策略保证集群内计算和集群间通信的安全性、可靠性和隐私性。 Nebula-I采用PaddlePaddle深度学习框架实现，可以支持异构硬件上的协作训练，例如： GPU 和 NPU。实验表明，所提出的框架可以最大限度地提高训练效率，同时保持令人满意的 NLP 性能。通过使用Nebula-I，用户可以以最少的开发在云集群上运行大规模的训练任务，并进一步提升现有大型预训练模型的效用。我们还介绍了跨语言自然语言推理任务的最新成果，这些成果是基于新颖的学习框架和 Nebula-I 生成的。 本文提出Nebula-I框架，用于在低带宽云集群上协作训练深度学习模型，解决跨集群训练中的通信效率与安全性问题。 Yang Xiang,Zhihua Wu,Weibao Gong,Siyu Ding,Xianjie Mo,Yuang Liu,Shuohuan Wang,Peng Liu,Yongshuai Hou,Long Li,Bin Wang,Shaohuai Shi,Yaqian Han,Yue Yu,Ge Li,Yu Sun,Yanjun Ma,Dianhai Yu cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC

title: Personalized Interventions for Online Moderation
title_zh: 在线审核的个性化干预
comment: The 33rd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media (HT '22)
comments: The 33rd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media (HT '22)
abstract: Current online moderation follows a one-size-fits-all approach, where each intervention is applied in the same way to all users. This naive approach is challenged by established socio-behavioral theories and by recent empirical results that showed the limited effectiveness of such interventions. We propose a paradigm-shift in online moderation by moving towards a personalized and user-centered approach. Our multidisciplinary vision combines state-of-the-art theories and practices in diverse fields such as computer science, sociology and psychology, to design personalized moderation interventions (PMIs). In outlining the path leading to the next-generation of moderation interventions, we also discuss the most prominent challenges introduced by such a disruptive change.
summary: Current online moderation follows a one-size-fits-all approach, where each intervention is applied in the same way to all users. This naive approach is challenged by established socio-behavioral theories and by recent empirical results that showed the limited effectiveness of such interventions. We propose a paradigm-shift in online moderation by moving towards a personalized and user-centered approach. Our multidisciplinary vision combines state-of-the-art theories and practices in diverse fields such as computer science, sociology and psychology, to design personalized moderation interventions (PMIs). In outlining the path leading to the next-generation of moderation interventions, we also discuss the most prominent challenges introduced by such a disruptive change.
abstract_zh: 当前的在线审核遵循一刀切的方法，其中每种干预措施都以相同的方式应用于所有用户。这种幼稚的方法受到既定的社会行为理论和最近的实证结果的挑战，这些结果表明此类干预措施的有效性有限。我们建议通过转向个性化和以用户为中心的方法来改变在线审核的范式。我们的多学科愿景结合了计算机科学、社会学和心理学等不同领域的最先进的理论和实践，以设计个性化的调节干预措施 (PMI)。在概述下一代适度干预措施的路径时，我们还讨论了这种颠覆性变化带来的最突出的挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于用户个性化的在线内容审核干预方法，旨在替代传统的统一干预模式。
AI.motivation: 传统在线审核的统一干预方式因社会行为理论和实证研究显示效果有限而受到挑战，需更符合用户特征的干预策略。
AI.method: 融合计算机科学、社会学与心理学理论，构建多学科框架设计个性化审核干预方案（PMIs）。
AI.result: 提出了下一代审核干预的发展路径，并阐述了该范式转变带来的主要技术与伦理挑战。
AI.conclusion: 个性化干预具有提升审核效果的潜力，但需解决算法偏见、用户隐私及跨平台一致性等关键问题。

title: Insights on Neural Representations for End-to-End Speech Recognition
title_zh: 端到端语音识别的神经表示的见解
abstract: End-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) models aim to learn a generalised speech representation. However, there are limited tools available to understand the internal functions and the effect of hierarchical dependencies within the model architecture. It is crucial to understand the correlations between the layer-wise representations, to derive insights on the relationship between neural representations and performance. Previous investigations of network similarities using correlation analysis techniques have not been explored for End-to-End ASR models. This paper analyses and explores the internal dynamics between layers during training with CNN, LSTM and Transformer based approaches using Canonical correlation analysis (CCA) and centered kernel alignment (CKA) for the experiments. It was found that neural representations within CNN layers exhibit hierarchical correlation dependencies as layer depth increases but this is mostly limited to cases where neural representation correlates more closely. This behaviour is not observed in LSTM architecture, however there is a bottom-up pattern observed across the training process, while Transformer encoder layers exhibit irregular coefficiency correlation as neural depth increases. Altogether, these results provide new insights into the role that neural architectures have upon speech recognition performance. More specifically, these techniques can be used as indicators to build better performing speech recognition models.
summary: End-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) models aim to learn a generalised speech representation. However, there are limited tools available to understand the internal functions and the effect of hierarchical dependencies within the model architecture. It is crucial to understand the correlations between the layer-wise representations, to derive insights on the relationship between neural representations and performance. Previous investigations of network similarities using correlation analysis techniques have not been explored for End-to-End ASR models. This paper analyses and explores the internal dynamics between layers during training with CNN, LSTM and Transformer based approaches using Canonical correlation analysis (CCA) and centered kernel alignment (CKA) for the experiments. It was found that neural representations within CNN layers exhibit hierarchical correlation dependencies as layer depth increases but this is mostly limited to cases where neural representation correlates more closely. This behaviour is not observed in LSTM architecture, however there is a bottom-up pattern observed across the training process, while Transformer encoder layers exhibit irregular coefficiency correlation as neural depth increases. Altogether, these results provide new insights into the role that neural architectures have upon speech recognition performance. More specifically, these techniques can be used as indicators to build better performing speech recognition models.
abstract_zh: 端到端自动语音识别（ASR）模型旨在学习广义语音表示。然而，可用于理解模型架构内的内部功能和分层依赖关系的影响的工具有限。了解分层表示之间的相关性对于深入了解神经表示和性能之间的关系至关重要。之前使用相关分析技术对网络相似性进行的研究尚未针对端到端 ASR 模型进行探索。本文使用典型相关分析 (CCA) 和中心核对齐 (CKA) 进行实验，分析和探索基于 CNN、LSTM 和 Transformer 的方法训练期间各层之间的内部动态。研究发现，随着层深度的增加，CNN 层内的神经表示表现出层次相关依赖性，但这主要限于神经表示关联更紧密的情况。在 LSTM 架构中没有观察到这种行为，但是在整个训练过程中观察到了自下而上的模式，而 Transformer 编码器层随着神经深度的增加表现出不规则的系数相关性。总而言之，这些结果为神经架构对语音识别性能的作用提供了新的见解。更具体地说，这些技术可以用作构建性能更好的语音识别模型的指标。

AI.tldr: 本文通过分析不同神经网络架构（CNN、LSTM、Transformer）在端到端语音识别中的内部表示动态，探讨其对模型性能的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有工具难以理解端到端语音识别模型的内部机制及层级依赖关系，需通过相关性分析揭示神经表示与性能的关系。
AI.method: 采用典型相关分析（CCA）和中心化核对齐（CKA）方法，研究CNN、LSTM和Transformer架构在训练过程中的层级表示动态。
AI.result: CNN层级间呈现深度增加的分层相关性，LSTM显示训练过程中的自底向上模式，Transformer编码器层级相关性则随深度增加呈不规则变化。
AI.conclusion: 不同架构的神经表示特性影响语音识别性能，相关分析技术可为优化模型设计提供依据。

ai_content_json.result: CNN层级间呈现深度增加的分层相关性，LSTM显示训练过程中的自底向上模式，Transformer编码器层级相关性则随深度增加呈不规则变化。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 不同架构的神经表示特性影响语音识别性能，相关分析技术可为优化模型设计提供依据。

tldr: 本文通过分析不同神经网络架构（CNN、LSTM、Transformer）在端到端语音识别中的内部表示动态，探讨其对模型性能的影响。

search_text: Insights on Neural Representations for End-to-End Speech Recognition 端到端语音识别的神经表示的见解 End-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) models aim to learn a generalised speech representation. However, there are limited tools available to understand the internal functions and the effect of hierarchical dependencies within the model architecture. It is crucial to understand the correlations between the layer-wise representations, to derive insights on the relationship between neural representations and performance. Previous investigations of network similarities using correlation analysis techniques have not been explored for End-to-End ASR models. This paper analyses and explores the internal dynamics between layers during training with CNN, LSTM and Transformer based approaches using Canonical correlation analysis (CCA) and centered kernel alignment (CKA) for the experiments. It was found that neural representations within CNN layers exhibit hierarchical correlation dependencies as layer depth increases but this is mostly limited to cases where neural representation correlates more closely. This behaviour is not observed in LSTM architecture, however there is a bottom-up pattern observed across the training process, while Transformer encoder layers exhibit irregular coefficiency correlation as neural depth increases. Altogether, these results provide new insights into the role that neural architectures have upon speech recognition performance. More specifically, these techniques can be used as indicators to build better performing speech recognition models. 端到端自动语音识别（ASR）模型旨在学习广义语音表示。然而，可用于理解模型架构内的内部功能和分层依赖关系的影响的工具有限。了解分层表示之间的相关性对于深入了解神经表示和性能之间的关系至关重要。之前使用相关分析技术对网络相似性进行的研究尚未针对端到端 ASR 模型进行探索。本文使用典型相关分析 (CCA) 和中心核对齐 (CKA) 进行实验，分析和探索基于 CNN、LSTM 和 Transformer 的方法训练期间各层之间的内部动态。研究发现，随着层深度的增加，CNN 层内的神经表示表现出层次相关依赖性，但这主要限于神经表示关联更紧密的情况。在 LSTM 架构中没有观察到这种行为，但是在整个训练过程中观察到了自下而上的模式，而 Transformer 编码器层随着神经深度的增加表现出不规则的系数相关性。总而言之，这些结果为神经架构对语音识别性能的作用提供了新的见解。更具体地说，这些技术可以用作构建性能更好的语音识别模型的指标。 本文通过分析不同神经网络架构（CNN、LSTM、Transformer）在端到端语音识别中的内部表示动态，探讨其对模型性能的影响。 Anna Ollerenshaw,Md Asif Jalal,Thomas Hain cs.CL,cs.SD,eess.AS

title: Differential Privacy: What is all the noise about?
title_zh: 差异隐私：所有这些噪音是关于什么的？
abstract: Differential Privacy (DP) is a formal definition of privacy that provides rigorous guarantees against risks of privacy breaches during data processing. It makes no assumptions about the knowledge or computational power of adversaries, and provides an interpretable, quantifiable and composable formalism. DP has been actively researched during the last 15 years, but it is still hard to master for many Machine Learning (ML)) practitioners. This paper aims to provide an overview of the most important ideas, concepts and uses of DP in ML, with special focus on its intersection with Federated Learning (FL).
summary: Differential Privacy (DP) is a formal definition of privacy that provides rigorous guarantees against risks of privacy breaches during data processing. It makes no assumptions about the knowledge or computational power of adversaries, and provides an interpretable, quantifiable and composable formalism. DP has been actively researched during the last 15 years, but it is still hard to master for many Machine Learning (ML)) practitioners. This paper aims to provide an overview of the most important ideas, concepts and uses of DP in ML, with special focus on its intersection with Federated Learning (FL).
abstract_zh: 差分隐私（DP）是隐私的正式定义，它为数据处理过程中的隐私泄露风险提供了严格的保证。它不对对手的知识或计算能力做出任何假设，并提供可解释、可量化和可组合的形式主义。 DP 在过去 15 年里得到了积极的研究，但对于许多机器学习 (ML) 从业者来说仍然很难掌握。本文旨在概述 ML 中 DP 的最重要思想、概念和用途，特别关注其与联邦学习 (FL) 的交叉点。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在概述差分隐私（DP）在机器学习（ML）中的核心概念、应用及与联邦学习（FL）的交叉点，帮助ML从业者掌握DP。
AI.motivation: 尽管DP研究已有15年，但其复杂性使许多ML从业者难以掌握，需通过系统性综述降低理解门槛并促进实际应用。
AI.method: 通过回顾DP的理论基础、隐私保证机制及在FL中的具体应用，结合案例分析与概念解析，梳理其关键思想与挑战。
AI.result: 阐明了DP的核心原理与隐私保护机制，明确了其在FL中的作用，并指出现有研究的局限性及未来方向。
AI.conclusion: DP为ML提供了可量化的隐私保障框架，但需进一步简化工具与理论，以推动其在FL等场景中的广泛落地。
